preamble: The shift from the voiced dental fricative [ð] to the high-front vowel [i] (or palatal glide [j]) is a form of vocalization. It occurs in specific phonetic environments where the weak fricative loses its dental contact and consolidates into a palatal resonance, typically after front vowels.

examples.note: Vocalization of the 'soft d' into a palatal glide/vowel quality in informal registers.
Sporadic reduction where the final dental fricative is absorbed into a vowel-like glide.
